citation.title: ANCA-associated vasculitis—treatment standard
citation.source_type: Review Article - European League of Associations of Rheumatology (EULAR) Guidelines

key_takeaways.insight: Induction treatment of life/organ-threatening AAV is a combination of glucocorticoids and rituximab or cyclophosphamide, with rituximab preferred for relapsing GPA/MPA
Rapidly reducing glucocorticoid regimen (PEXIVAS schedule) reduces serious infection risk without loss of efficacy; plasma exchange beneficial in serum creatinine >300 µmol/L
Fixed-interval rituximab (500 mg every 6 months for 24-48 months) superior to azathioprine/methotrexate for maintenance, permits glucocorticoid discontinuation within 6 months
Avacopan (C5a receptor antagonist) superior to standard glucocorticoid tapering when combined with rituximab/cyclophosphamide; shows faster proteinuria reduction and improved kidney recovery
Mepolizumab (anti-IL-5) permits glucocorticoid reduction, improves remission and reduces relapse in EGPA; benefit maintained beyond 1 year
Delayed remission associates with higher mortality and ESKD risk; prompt diagnosis and rapid treatment initiation critical for preventing organ damage
Treatment toxicities and comorbidities (infections, cardiovascular disease, cancer) dominate long-term management; require personalized treatment strategies based on patient characteristics

aav_subtypes.name: Granulomatosis with Polyangiitis
aav_subtypes.characteristics: Small-vessel necrotizing vasculitis with granulomatous inflammation; typically affects respiratory tract, kidneys, and other organs

aav_subtypes.name: Microscopic Polyangiitis
aav_subtypes.characteristics: Small-vessel necrotizing vasculitis without granulomas; predominantly renal involvement

aav_subtypes.name: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis with Polyangiitis
aav_subtypes.characteristics: Necrotizing vasculitis with eosinophilic granulomas; asthma, eosinophilia, and neuropathy prominent

immunosuppressives.mechanism: Anti-CD20 B-cell depleting monoclonal antibody; central role in pathogenesis via ANCA-producing plasma cells and antigen presentation
induction_dosing: IV 375 mg/m² once weekly for 4 doses
maintenance_dosing: IV 500 mg at Weeks 0 and 2, then 6-monthly for 24-48 months
immunosuppressives.precautions: Removal by PLEX; prophylaxis TMP-SMX; avoid third trimester pregnancy; monitor for immune deficiency
immunosuppressives.efficacy_notes: Preferred for relapsing disease; some treatment-resistant cases with anti-globulin response; B-cell return correlates with relapse risk

immunosuppressives.mechanism: Alkylating agent causing B and T cell suppression
induction_dosing: IV 15 mg/kg at Weeks 0, 2, 4, 7, 10, 13 (can extend to 16, 19, 21, 24)
immunosuppressives.precautions: Dose reduction if age >60 or GFR <30; avoid first 10 weeks gestation; IV preferred to oral due to reduced bladder toxicity
immunosuppressives.efficacy_notes: IV preferred; low-dose IV 15 mg/kg at weeks 0 and 2 effective when combined with rituximab

immunosuppressives.mechanism: Oral C5a receptor (C5aR1) antagonist; targets complement alternative pathway
immunosuppressives.precautions: Unknown safety in pregnancy and beyond 1 year; not recommended if GFR <15 mL/min/1.73 m²
immunosuppressives.efficacy_notes: Superior to standard glucocorticoid taper in ADVOCATE trial; faster proteinuria reduction and improved kidney recovery; sustained GFR improvement at 52 weeks

immunosuppressives.mechanism: Anti-interleukin-5 monoclonal antibody; targets eosinophil development and survival
dosing: SC 100 mg every 4 weeks (approved dose for severe eosinophilic asthma)
immunosuppressives.efficacy_notes: Effective in MIRRA trial; reduces steroid exposure; improves remission and reduces relapse in EGPA; benefit in ANCA-negative and ANCA-positive EGPA; sustained beyond 1 year

immunosuppressives.mechanism: Purine analog; inhibits T and B cell proliferation
immunosuppressives.precautions: Check TPMT; reduce sun exposure; safe in pregnancy
immunosuppressives.efficacy_notes: Less effective than rituximab for maintenance; inferior to rituximab in MAINRITSAN and RITAZAREM trials

immunosuppressives.mechanism: Antifolate; inhibits DNA synthesis and T cell proliferation
monitoring: Kidney function (dose reduction at GFR 30-60, discontinue at GFR <30)
immunosuppressives.precautions: Folic acid prophylaxis (5 mg/week); contraindicated in pregnancy; renal function dependent dosing
immunosuppressives.efficacy_notes: Limited success as alternative; less effective than rituximab

immunosuppressives.mechanism: Inosine monophosphate dehydrogenase inhibitor; selective T and B cell suppression
immunosuppressives.dosing: Oral 2000 mg/day (divided doses, may increase to 3000 mg/day)
immunosuppressives.efficacy_notes: Limited success; alternative to rituximab for maintenance

immunosuppressives.mechanism: Polyclonal immunoglobulin; multiple mechanisms including FcγR blockade
immunosuppressives.efficacy_notes: Limited evidence; used in refractory disease

induction_dosing: Taper according to PEXIVAS reduced schema
corticosteroids.efficacy_notes: PEXIVAS trial showed reduced-dose regimen equivalent to standard regimen with fewer infections; rapid taper associated with better outcomes

adjunctive_therapies.indication: Severe renal disease (serum creatinine >300 µmol/L) or diffuse alveolar hemorrhage
adjunctive_therapies.efficacy: PEXIVAS trial: did not improve death/ESKD endpoint; meta-analysis showed reduced ESKD risk at 12 months in high-risk subgroups

adjunctive_therapies.benefit: Also reduces general bacterial infection frequency

clinical_trials.full_name: Plasma Exchange and Glucocorticoids in Severe ANCA-Associated Vasculitis
clinical_trials.patient_population: 704 AAV patients with GFR <50 mL/min/1.73 m² or lung hemorrhage
clinical_trials.intervention: Plasma exchange vs standard glucocorticoid taper; reduced-dose vs standard-dose glucocorticoids
key_findings: No significant difference in composite endpoint death/ESKD
Reduced-dose glucocorticoid regimen as effective as standard with 40% lower cumulative exposure
Fewer serious infections with reduced-dose regimen
Meta-analysis: plasma exchange reduced ESKD risk at 12 months in high-risk subgroups (serum creatinine >300 µmol/L)
clinical_trials.clinical_impact: Established PEXIVAS glucocorticoid tapering schedule as new standard

clinical_trials.full_name: Rituximab versus cyclophosphamide for ANCA-Associated Vasculitis
key_findings: Rituximab effective as alternative to cyclophosphamide for induction
Preferred for relapsing disease

clinical_trials.full_name: Rituximab versus azathioprine for maintenance of remission for patients with ANCA-associated vasculitis and relapsing disease
key_findings: Rituximab superior to azathioprine for maintenance
Supports rituximab as first-line maintenance therapy

clinical_trials.full_name: Rituximab versus Cyclophosphamide in ANCA-Associated Renal Vasculitis
key_findings: Low-dose cyclophosphamide (500 mg every 2 weeks x6) combined with rituximab as effective as full-dose cyclophosphamide
Steroid-sparing in observational follow-up studies

clinical_trials.full_name: Rituximab versus Azathioprine for Maintenance in ANCA-Associated Vasculitis
key_findings: Rituximab superior to azathioprine for maintenance therapy
Supports rituximab as preferred maintenance approach

clinical_trials.full_name: Long-Term Rituximab Use to Maintain Remission of Antineutrophil Cytoplasmic Antibody-Associated Vasculitis
key_findings: Supports extended rituximab use (2-4 years) for maintenance
Benefit of prolonged remission maintenance therapy

clinical_trials.full_name: Randomised controlled trial of prolonged treatment in the remission phase of ANCA-associated vasculitis
key_findings: Supports extending maintenance treatment from 2 to 4 years
Azathioprine and prednisolone study; benefit of prolonged duration

clinical_trials.full_name: Avacopan for the Treatment of ANCA-Associated Vasculitis
clinical_trials.intervention: Avacopan 30 mg BID vs glucocorticoid tapering (withdrawal at week 21)
key_findings: Avacopan superior for sustained remission at 12 months
Improved quality of life (SF-36)
Faster reduction in urine albumin-creatinine ratio (40% by month 1)
Higher GFR recovery at 52 weeks (mean difference 3.2 mL/min/1.73 m²)
More pronounced GFR recovery in lower GFR subgroups (5.5 mL/min in GFR <30, 8.4 mL/min in GFR <20)
Fewer glucocorticoid-related complications
clinical_trials.clinical_impact: Avacopan approved; attractive alternative for glucocorticoid-intolerant patients

clinical_trials.full_name: Effect of Reduced-Dose vs High-Dose Glucocorticoids Added to Rituximab on Remission Induction in ANCA-Associated Vasculitis
key_findings: Direct comparison of glucocorticoid regimens
Supports reduced-dose glucocorticoid approach

clinical_trials.full_name: Rituximab versus Conventional therapeutic strategy for remission induction in eosinophilic granulomatosis with polyangiitis
key_findings: Rituximab non-inferior to cyclophosphamide for remission induction at 6 months
Tested with high-dose glucocorticoid tapering regimen

clinical_trials.full_name: Adding Azathioprine to Remission-Induction Glucocorticoids for Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis, Microscopic Polyangiitis, or Polyarteritis Nodosa Without Poor Prognosis Factors
key_findings: EGPA patients with FFS 0: azathioprine addition to glucocorticoids did not reduce relapse risk

clinical_trials.full_name: Mepolizumab or Placebo for Eosinophilic Granulomatosis with Polyangiitis
key_findings: Mepolizumab 300 mg every 4 weeks effective in prevalent EGPA
Reduced steroid exposure
Improved remission rates
Reduced relapse risk
Benefit in ANCA-negative and ANCA-positive EGPA
Sustained benefit beyond 1 year

clinical_trials.full_name: Efficacy and Safety of Belimumab and Azathioprine for Maintenance of Remission in Antineutrophil Cytoplasmic Antibody-Associated Vasculitis
key_findings: Belimumab (BLyS inhibitor) failed to demonstrate relapse reduction
Trial design issues and low placebo event rate
Still investigating synergistic effect with rituximab

clinical_trials.full_name: Randomised study of rituximab and belimumab sequential therapy in PR3 ANCA-associated vasculitis
clinical_trials.objective: Assess synergistic B-cell depletion effect of rituximab + belimumab combination

clinical_trials.full_name: Obinutuzumab compared with rituximab for treating ANCA-associated vasculitis
clinical_trials.rationale: Obinutuzumab shows superior direct cell death and ADCC; more complete B-cell depletion in lupus nephritis trials

clinical_trials.full_name: Abatacept (CTLA4-Ig) for the Treatment of Relapsing, Non Severe, Granulomatosis with Polyangiitis
clinical_trials.rationale: Abatacept modulates T-cell costimulatory signal; promising open-label results in non-severe GPA with high remission and steroid-sparing effect

clinical_trials.full_name: Efficacy and Safety of Benralizumab in EGPA Compared to Mepolizumab
Efficacy and Safety of Depemokimab Compared With Mepolizumab in Adults With Relapsing or Refractory EGPA

pathophysiology.description: PR3-ANCA and MPO-ANCA are pathogenic antibodies that activate neutrophils and promote necrotizing vasculitis
pathophysiology.clinical_relevance: ANCA serotype associates with disease phenotype and organ involvement

pathophysiology.description: B cells central to AAV pathogenesis as ANCA-producing plasma cells and antigen-presenting cells supporting T-cell activation
pathophysiology.key_mechanism: B-cell-activating factor/lymphocyte stimulator (BAFF/BLyS) crucial for B-cell development and function

pathophysiology.description: Critical pathway for ANCA vasculitis demonstrated in experimental models
pathophysiology.activation_status: Elevated in active disease; reduced with remission
pathophysiology.key_molecule: C5a is potent neutrophil chemotactic factor and activator

pathophysiology.description: Genetic variants in MHC and non-MHC genes associate with AAV susceptibility and characteristics
pathophysiology.mhc_associations: Differential associations between PR3-ANCA and MPO-ANCA subtypes
pathophysiology.note: HLA-DQ associated with MPO-ANCA EGPA; ANCA-negative EGPA related to mucosal response genes

pathophysiology.description: T cells play crucial role in pathogenesis
pathophysiology.mechanisms: Modulates costimulatory signal required for T-cell activation

pathophysiology.description: IL-5 central to eosinophil development, survival, and recruitment
GPA characterized by granulomatous lesions with B-cell and T-cell infiltration
pathophysiology.clinical_implication: Some patients resistant to rituximab, particularly in granulomatous disease

disease_definitions.definition: Presence of features of disease activity (clinical, serological, or histological)
disease_definitions.monitoring: BVAS (Birmingham Vasculitis Activity Score), CRP, ESR, ANCA levels

disease_definitions.definition: Absence of active disease while on low prednisolone dose (usually ≤10 mg/day) after 3-6 months induction
Stabilization of kidney function and resolution of active urinary findings
disease_definitions.note: Persistent hematuria increases relapse risk; persistent proteinuria (≥0.05 g/mmol) associates with worse long-term GFR

treatment_principles.importance: Diagnostic delay contributes to poor outcomes, especially renal involvement
treatment_principles.goal: Initiate high-suspicion cases while pending confirmatory biopsy

treatment_principles.importance: Delayed remission associates with higher mortality and ESKD risk
treatment_principles.strategy: Combination of glucocorticoids + rituximab or cyclophosphamide
Use PEXIVAS reduced-dose regimen; target rapid taper to minimize toxicity
treatment_principles.benefit: Reduced serious infections without loss of efficacy

treatment_principles.strategy: Continue maintenance for 2-4 years with rituximab preferred
treatment_principles.goal: Prevent relapse (>50% relapse rate despite maintenance)

treatment_principles.importance: Over 50% of AAV patients relapse during disease course

treatment_principles.strategy: TMP-SMX prophylaxis; vaccination timing optimization; immunoglobulin monitoring
Screen for cardiovascular factors (diabetes, lipids, BP); manage osteoporosis
Consider patient characteristics (age, fertility, comorbidities) in drug selection
treatment_principles.examples: Rituximab over cyclophosphamide for elderly/fertile patients; avacopan for high glucocorticoid toxicity risk

first_line.preferred_scenario: Rituximab for relapsing disease; cyclophosphamide for first-presentation severe disease
first_line.glucocorticoid_dosing: PEXIVAS reduced-dose regimen with rapid taper
first_line.cyclophosphamide_dosing: IV 15 mg/kg at Weeks 0, 2, 4, 7, 10, 13 (extend as needed)

options: Rituximab 1000 mg Week 0,2 + Cyclophosphamide 15 mg/kg Week 0,2
Rituximab 1000 mg Week 0,2 + Cyclophosphamide 500 mg every 2 weeks x6
combination_approach.advantage: Lower morbidity with efficacy comparable to full-dose cyclophosphamide

avacopan_approach.components: Avacopan 30 mg BID + rituximab or cyclophosphamide
avacopan_approach.glucocorticoid_strategy: Rapid taper and withdrawal by week 4

plasma_exchange.indication: Serum creatinine >300 µmol/L (3.39 mg/dL) OR diffuse alveolar hemorrhage with hypoxia
plasma_exchange.consideration: Increased infection risk must be weighed against benefits

dosing: IV 500 mg at Weeks 0 and 2, then 6-monthly for 24-48 months
first_line.glucocorticoid_goal: Discontinuation within 6 months of induction start

second_line_alternatives.comment: Usually with low-dose glucocorticoids (discontinuation increases relapse)

treatment_duration_decision.evidence_base: MAINRITSAN III supports extended treatment; REMAIN supports 4-year duration

induction_poor_prognosis.evidence: Favorable long-term prognosis reported in 70-patient cohort with cardiac involvement

induction_good_prognosis.alternative: Rituximab as non-inferior option (REOVAS trial, abstract)

dosing: OR SC 100 mg every 4 weeks (severe asthma approved dose)

second_line.note: Glucocorticoid reduction limited by frequent relapses; immunosuppressives often used
agents: Rituximab (limited efficacy vs GPA/MPA; observational data only)
second_line.consideration: Rituximab less beneficial in EGPA compared to GPA/MPA

emerging_agents.note: Efficacy in open-label EGPA pilots; comparison with mepolizumab ongoing
Initial efficacy in open-label EGPA pilots

special_considerations.disease_phenotype: Much improvement with anti-IL-5 reflects asthma/naso-sinus disease control
special_considerations.severe_vasculitis: Insufficient data for mepolizumab in severe presentations with organ failure

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: No widely accepted subgrouping system for patient stratification
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.terminology: Severe/non-severe, major/minor, organ-threatening/non-organ-threatening used without robust definitions
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.clinical_impact: Confusing for treatment decisions; no standardized approach to therapy escalation

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: Reduced-dose regimen from PEXIVAS not formally tested with rituximab
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.context: PEXIVAS predominantly used cyclophosphamide; RAVE trial used higher glucocorticoid doses
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.clinical_impact: Unclear if PEXIVAS reduced regimen appropriate for rituximab induction

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: PEXIVAS trial did not improve primary endpoint (death/ESKD); meta-analysis shows benefit only in subgroups
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.evidence: Reduced ESKD risk at 12 months in serum creatinine >300 µmol/L subgroup
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.controversy: Unclear which patients benefit; requires balancing against infection risk

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: No clinical trial data beyond 1 year
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.concern: Unknown safety profile for extended use beyond 12 months
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.contraindication: Not recommended if GFR <15 mL/min/1.73 m²
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.clinical_impact: Limits use in severely renal-compromised patients and long-term management

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: Small proportion of patients fail to achieve remission despite rituximab
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.mechanism: Possibly early relapses and anti-globulin antibody response
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.clinical_impact: Predictors and rescue strategies not well-defined

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: PR3/MPO-ANCA and CD19+ B cells helpful but imperfect
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.limitation: Low assay sensitivity/specificity and frequency of testing prevents reliable relapse prevention
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.evidence: ANCA levels and B-cell return most helpful after rituximab withdrawal

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: Fixed regimen vs biomarker-guided dosing not established
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.consideration: Goal of achieving low total cumulative dose for safety while maintaining efficacy
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.evidence_gap: No standardized approach to individualizing maintenance duration

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: MIRRA trial primarily assessed asthma/naso-sinus disease improvement
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.gap: Insufficient data for severe vasculitic presentations with organ failure
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.clinical_implication: Cannot recommend monotherapy mepolizumab for severe EGPA vasculitis

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: Belimumab (anti-BAFF) failed to show relapse reduction alone (BREVAS trial)
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.ongoing_investigation: Synergistic effect of belimumab + rituximab in COMBIVAS trial
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.rationale: Dual B-cell pathway targeting may improve outcomes

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: Abatacept promising in open-label studies; RCT (ABROGATE) ongoing
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.gap: Limited evidence for T-cell modulation as standard therapy

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: Vilobelimab (anti-C5a) showed promise in phase 2 trial (abstract)
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.gap: No phase 3 RCT data yet; comparison with avacopan needed

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: Berden classification, MCCS, and ARRS risk stratification scores developed
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.limitation: Cannot guide individual treatment decisions; even high-risk patients may have renal recovery
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.research_direction: Refinement needed for precise interventions based on histology findings

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.topic: Urinary biomarkers of renal disease activity
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.issue: New biomarkers (soluble CD163, MCP-1) under investigation
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.uncertainty: Whether urinary findings represent active disease or irreversible damage
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.limitation: Lack of repeat biopsy validation studies

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.ongoing: ACR/EULAR developing consensus composite response criteria for clinical trials
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.gap: No universally accepted, validated remission criteria for clinical practice
Limited data on safety and efficacy in these subgroups; many trials exclude these populations
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.clinical_need: Robust evidence for personalized treatment strategies

evidence_gaps_and_controversies.limitation: Rarely opportunity to immunize before induction
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.approach: Optimization during maintenance (≥4 weeks before next rituximab dose)
evidence_gaps_and_controversies.gap: Need for better vaccination strategies in immunosuppressed AAV patients

data_quality_notes.source_quality: High-quality review article based on randomized controlled trials and EULAR 2022 guidelines
data_quality_notes.trial_status_note: Some trials listed as 'abstract' or 'ongoing' indicate ongoing studies at publication date
data_quality_notes.accessibility: Source is open-access PMC article with full text available
